segments.text: Then Jacob called for his sons and said:
Gather around so I can tell you what will happen to you in days to come.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Assemble and listen, sons of Jacob; listen to your father Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might, the first sign of my strength, excelling in honor, excelling in power.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Turbulent as the waters, you will no longer excel, for you went up onto your father's bed, onto my couch and defiled it.
Simeon and Levi are brothers— their swords are weapons of violence.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Let me not enter their council, let me not join their assembly, for they have killed men in their anger and hamstrung oxen as they pleased.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Cursed be their anger, so fierce, and their fury, so cruel! I will scatter them in Jacob and disperse them in Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Judah, your brothers will praise you; your hand will be on the neck of your enemies; your father's sons will bow down to you.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: You are a lion's cub, Judah; you return from the prey, my son. Like a lion he crouches and lies down, like a lioness—who dares to rouse him?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until he to whom it belongs shall come and the obedience of the nations shall be his.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He will tether his donkey to a vine, his colt to the choicest branch; he will wash his garments in wine, his robes in the blood of grapes.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: His eyes will be darker than wine, his teeth whiter than milk.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Zebulun will live by the seashore and become a haven for ships; his border will extend toward Sidon.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Issachar is a rawboned donkey lying down among the sheep pens.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: When he sees how good is his resting place and how pleasant is his land, he will bend his shoulder to the burden and submit to forced labor.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Dan will provide justice for his people as one of the tribes of Israel.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Dan will be a snake by the roadside, a viper along the path, that bites the horse's heels so that its rider tumbles backward.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I look for your deliverance, Lord.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Gad will be attacked by a band of raiders, but he will attack them at their heels.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Asher's food will be rich; he will provide delicacies fit for a king.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Naphtali is a doe set free that bears beautiful fawns.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Joseph is a fruitful vine, a fruitful vine near a spring, whose branches climb over a wall.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: With bitterness archers attacked him; they shot at him with hostility.
But his bow remained steady, his strong arms stayed limber, because of the hand of the Mighty One of Jacob, because of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel,
because of your father's God, who helps you, because of the Almighty, who blesses you with blessings of the skies above, blessings of the deep springs below, blessings of the breast and womb.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Your father's blessings are greater than the blessings of the ancient mountains, than the bounty of the age-old hills. Let all these rest on the head of Joseph, on the brow of the prince among his brothers.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf; in the morning he devours the prey, in the evening he divides the plunder.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said to them when he blessed them, giving each the blessing appropriate to him.
Then he gave them these instructions:
I am about to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my fathers in the cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: the cave in the field of Machpelah, near Mamre in Canaan, which Abraham bought along with the field as a burial place from Ephron the Hittite.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: There Abraham and his wife Sarah were buried, there Isaac and his wife Rebekah were buried, and there I buried Leah.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: The field and the cave in it were bought from the Hittites.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: When Jacob had finished giving instructions to his sons, he drew his feet up into the bed, breathed his last and was gathered to his people.
